sentences.text: Everyone went to little Hans' funeral because he was popular, and the Miller was the chief mourner.
The Miller said he was Hans' best friend, so he walked at the front and wiped his eyes with a big handkerchief.
‘Little Hans is a big loss to everyone,’ said the Blacksmith.
They sat in the inn, drank wine, and ate cakes.
